verses.text: God calls Moses from the tent to teach Israel how to bring their offerings. The call. Worship is on God's terms, not man's.
God calls Moses from the tent to teach Israel how to bring their offerings. The call. Worship is on God's terms, not man's.
A burnt offering of the herd is brought to the door; the offerer lays his hand on its head, and it is killed. Burnt offering. The victim dies in the sinner's place.
A burnt offering of the herd is brought to the door; the offerer lays his hand on its head, and it is killed. Burnt offering. The victim dies in the sinner's place.
A burnt offering of the herd is brought to the door; the offerer lays his hand on its head, and it is killed. Burnt offering. The victim dies in the sinner's place.
It is flayed, cut, and wholly burnt, 'a sweet savour unto the LORD.' Wholly given. Total devotion ascends to God.
It is flayed, cut, and wholly burnt, 'a sweet savour unto the LORD.' Wholly given. Total devotion ascends to God.
It is flayed, cut, and wholly burnt, 'a sweet savour unto the LORD.' Wholly given. Total devotion ascends to God.
It is flayed, cut, and wholly burnt, 'a sweet savour unto the LORD.' Wholly given. Total devotion ascends to God.
A burnt offering of the flock is offered likewise. The flock. The poorer may bring a sheep or goat.
A burnt offering of the flock is offered likewise. The flock. The poorer may bring a sheep or goat.
A burnt offering of the flock is offered likewise. The flock. The poorer may bring a sheep or goat.
A burnt offering of the flock is offered likewise. The flock. The poorer may bring a sheep or goat.
A burnt offering of fowls is allowed for the poorest. The fowls. None is too poor to draw near to God.
A burnt offering of fowls is allowed for the poorest. The fowls. None is too poor to draw near to God.
